'''Michael Withers''' (born 16 May 1976) is a former [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|fb|wg}} and {{rlp|ce}} in the 1990s and 2000s. He played club football in Australia for the [[Balmain Tigers]] in the [[National Rugby League]], and in England with the [[Wigan Warriors]] and the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Withers was born in [[Sydney, New South Wales]], [[Australia]].

==Playing career==
===Australia===
While attending John Paul II Senior High School in [[Marayong, New South Wales]], Withers was selected for the [[Australia national schoolboy rugby league team|Australian Schoolboys team]] in 1994.<ref>{{cite web|publisher=Sporting Pulse|title=Australian Secondary Schools Rugby League|url=http://www.sportingpulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26424|access-date=10 October 2008}}</ref> In the same year Withers won the Australian [[List of Dally M Awards Winners#1994|Dally M]] ''Junior Player of the Year''.

===England===
Withers played for [[Bradford Bulls]] at centre in the [[1999 Super League Grand Final]] which was lost to [[St Helens R.F.C.]] Withers qualified for Great Britain through his Irish grandparents and he played for Ireland in the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup|2000 World Cup]]. He played for the [[Bradford Bulls]] at {{rlp|fb}} and scored 3 [[Try (rugby)|tries]] in their [[2001 Super League Grand Final]] victory against the [[Wigan Warriors]]. As a result, he was awarded the [[Harry Sunderland Trophy]]. He is also the only player to have recorded a hat-trick in a Grand Final, doing so against Wigan Warriors for Bradford Bulls in 2001.

Withers was selected for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] in the [[2001 Rugby league Ashes series|2001 Ashes Series]], but did not play due to injury concerns.<ref>{{cite news|format=fee required|publisher=AAP Sports News|title=Withers out of Australia Test|url=http://www.highbeam.com/doc/1P1-48332063.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110516231646/http://www.highbeam.com/doc/1P1-48332063.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=16 May 2011|access-date=1 January 2008|date=20 November 2001}}</ref> Withers later withdrew himself from the Great Britain squad without playing a game.<ref>{{cite news|format=fee required|publisher=AAP Sports News|title=Withers turns his back on Britain|url=http://www.highbeam.com/doc/1P1-49080198.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110516231718/http://www.highbeam.com/doc/1P1-49080198.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=16 May 2011|access-date=1 January 2008|date=22 December 2001}}</ref> His initial selection in the Great Britain training squad by Australian coach [[David Waite]] created some controversy.<ref>{{cite news|format=fee required|publisher=AAP Sports News|title=Australian picked in Britain squad for Kangaroos Test series|url=http://www.highbeam.com/doc/1P1-47600436.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110516231824/http://www.highbeam.com/doc/1P1-47600436.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=16 May 2011|access-date=1 January 2008|date=18 October 2001}}</ref>

As [[Super League VI]] champions, the Bulls played against [[2001 NRL season|2001 NRL]] Premiers, the [[Newcastle Knights]] in the [[2002 World Club Challenge]]. Withers played fullback and scored two tries in Bradford's victory. Withers played for the [[Bradford Bulls]] at {{rlp|fb}}, scoring a [[Try (rugby)|try]] in their [[2002 Super League Grand Final]] loss against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]. Withers played for the [[Bradford Bulls]] at centre in their [[2003 Super League Grand Final]] victory against the [[Wigan Warriors]]. Having won [[Super League VIII]], Bradford played against 2003 NRL Premiers, the [[Penrith Panthers]] in the [[2004 World Club Challenge]]. Withers played at {{rlp|fb}} in the Bulls' 22–4 victory. He also played for Bradford at fullback in their [[2004 Super League Grand Final]] loss against the [[Leeds Rhinos]]. Withers played for the [[Bradford Bulls]] at {{rlp|fb}} in their [[2005 Super League Grand Final]] victory against [[Leeds Rhinos]]. He scored 108 tries in 170 games for the Bulls. He has won three Grand Finals and two Challenge Cups with the Bulls. As Super League champions Bradford faced [[National Rugby League]] premiers [[Wests Tigers]] in the [[2006 World Club Challenge]]. Withers played at fullback in the Bulls' 30–10 victory.

Withers made his Wigan début in a 16–10 defeat by [[Warrington Wolves]] at the [[JJB Stadium]] on 9 February 2007. In June 2007 a knee injury forced Withers to retire.<ref name="Withers retirement stuns Warriors">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/wigan/6244482.stm|title=Withers retirement stuns Warriors|publisher=BBC|date=27 June 2007|access-date=27 June 2007}}</ref><ref name="Michael Withers Forced To Retire">{{cite web|url=http://www.wiganwarriors.com/news_full.asp?newsid=3007 |title=Michael Withers Forced To Retire |publisher=Wiganwarriors.com |date=27 June 2007 |access-date=27 June 2007 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070927213253/http://www.wiganwarriors.com/news_full.asp?newsid=3007 |archive-date=27 September 2007 |url-status=dead }}</ref>
